title: Asynchronous Architecture and Pattern Selection · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs asynchronous architecture and pattern selection from bounded publish, route, delivery, acknowledgement, and recovery evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the pattern selection and coupling decision record. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for pattern selection and coupling decision record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value.
Distinguish competing consumers on one queue from publish/subscribe using one queue per subscriber responsibility.
Evaluate request/reply over messaging against ordinary HTTP using correlation, timeout, cancellation, failure ambiguity, and capacity evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Temporal Coupling Decision
steps.instruction:
Order the Temporal Coupling Decision RabbitMQ and TypeScript HTTP evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Temporal Coupling Decision.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Temporal Coupling Decision assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Temporal Coupling Decision, keep Temporal Coupling Decision connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.text: A review messaging team handles case 4791 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose HTTP, work queue, pub/sub, or retained stream from explicit coupling needs; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose asynchronous messaging only when temporal decoupling, buffering, fan-out, failure isolation, or replay creates measurable value.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Temporal Coupling Decision has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
